Hey guys and welcome back. This is theme plate 14, the blind eye, and it's going to be pretty similar to the point of no return theme plate that we just explored previously, except this one is used to really fully release your prospect from those past failures and remind them of the responsibility to act on the better alternative now available to them. So it has this dynamic of coddling and then challenging and confronting. So it acts as that risk revealer and then putting them in a privileged place, a place of exclusive empowerment to do something about it. So here is how it breaks down. We begin by detailing the struggle, that moment of highest tension. Then we release them from blame, being very specific as to why it wasn't their fault. And then we will remind them of those non serving beliefs you set up during the relaunch, hold them accountable to the new version. You know, you've given them a new way, you've shown them a new path, you've flipped their paradigm, you've given them those a husband insights and now you get the right to close that loop. You know, it's not your fault until it is. That's where you kind of hold their feet to the fire, make them accountable for acting on what's now in front of them. Then you future pace the new outcomes and how your program facilitates it, remind them once again of the commitments they made earlier and close that consistency loop. So you opened up those consistency loop in the confirmation and in the prelaunch and now you're closing it. You're using that not your fault until it is dynamic. So here is how it plays out. Real talk and a VIP invitation. Almost every other day I get a teary eyed Facebook message from a yoga teacher kicking herself for spending years barely scraping by doing everything in her power to make it a go despite the silent doubts from friends and family hustling around town to teach classes at any studio that will have her scrolling through the glossy Instagram quote cards from her favorite yoga crushes wondering what the heck must be wrong with her. Why after all these years am I still stuck riding the bus to get to a gig I don't even want to teach. So this is that struggle in the rear view all that moment of highest tension stuff that we're bringing back to the surface and getting them intimate with ouch. Let's stop the self blame for a sec. Because here's the thing. It wasn't your fault that your YTT didn't prepare you for the business world that it had you believing there was only one revenue stream available to you and it required negotiating with stingy studio owners to get a piece of an ever shrinking pie or that it led you to the conclusion that it all it takes is a few crystals and mantras in the right sequencing between them to magically land a slew of high paying private clients that allow you to make teaching a full time thing. Nope. Can't be blamed for just playing with the script you were handed. So here's where we are releasing them from all that blame but more importantly qualifying why explaining why it wasn't their fault in a believable and a sincere way. But if the current definition of being a yoga teacher isn't serving you and hasn't been for a while, you are responsible for graduating into a new definition. So this is where the accountability comes into play. One that empowers, one that enriches, one that takes your lofty intentions and grounds them in the fertile soil of proven online marketing strategies that have fueled the rapid rise of all your favorite yoga crushes. You are responsible for learning about the nine revenue streams available to you, four of which are fully automated and passive. So here we're actually bringing elements of the program linking to how linking your program has a thing that facilitates this new paradigm they are responsible for stepping into. So there's that seamless integration, that recontextualization between the beliefs and paradigm they've shifted into and your program as being the only logical thing that provides it. And you are responsible for choosing the one that syncs up best with your authentic expression will help with that in module three and making use of the six group coaching calls to keep you on track. As for the first time since you began this yoga journey, you truly feel a sense of wow, I got this. In short, you're responsible for your own awesomeness. So holding them accountable again, this email is really high on holding your prospect to that standard, to that standard of being responsible for their own outcomes and how you share it with others in the sustainable way that serves all over the last few days, you've steeped in this new definition, you've sketched out some very real projections for your business and met the new version of you capable of carrying them out. Here's your invitation to flow forward and leave the hustle behind. So that is it that call to action that holds them accountable. Once again, this is a pretty similar email to the one above, you know, the point of no return except this one has that dynamic of coddling them from it's not your fault until it is, you know, it's not your fault, you couldn't have known better, this is just when you were handed. But now that you've integrated these beliefs now that you've seen a better way, you absolutely are responsible for leveraging them and acting upon them, our program, the thing we offer helps you capitalize on that new paradigm and that's the only one that fits that new reality that you've been initiated into. So that is the full dynamic of the of the blind eye theme plate. So this is one of my favorites as well. It's usually a pretty short one to write. So so yeah, give it a shot, take about 20 minutes, do a sand timer thing, if you will put on some binaural beats, drink some coffee, do whatever it is you do to get into your zone and see if in about 20 to 30 minutes you can walk away with the first draft of an effective high converting blind eye email. So that is it. That is it for the sales sequence. I am losing my voice.
